key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $111482.0M less capital spending $12715.0M ÷ 15014.745M diluted shares per AAPL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251031_rpt20250927_acc0000320193_25_000079.htm
Mid-cycle iPhone refresh plus high-margin Services growth; modest buyback tailwind

open_questions: Confirm Services milestone band (**$0–$85/sh**) versus deferred revenue already in consolidated free cash flow.
China and antitrust reserve band (**−$45 to −$8/sh**) pending regulatory outcomes.
Live price and final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
thesis: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and sells consumer hardware (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables) and monetizes a global installed base through Services (App Store, iCloud, Apple Music, Apple Pay, advertising, and warranty). **[Fact]** Shares trade on Nasdaq (AAPL). **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated net sales were **$307.0 billion**, operating income **$133.1 billion**, and net income **$112.0 billion** (diluted earnings **$7.46** per share) per `AAPL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251031_rpt20250927_acc0000320193_25_000079.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 cash from operations was **$111.5 billion**, capital spending **$12.7 billion**, and free cash flow about **$98.8 billion** on roughly **15.0 billion** diluted shares.
why_market_wrong: The stock may trade as a perpetual quality compounder while hardware cycles and regulatory risk compress normalized free cash flow multiples. **[Inference]** Disaggregated Services net sales reached **$109.2 billion** in FY2025 (up from **$96.2 billion** in FY2024), yet generalists may still anchor on iPhone unit headlines rather than high-margin recurring revenue. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **$187** per share of filing-locked economic value versus a price near **$327**, suggesting the market prices ecosystem durability, buybacks, and Apple Intelligence optionality beyond what the conservative component schedule captures today. **[Inference]** Cash and marketable securities of **$132.4 billion** against **$90.7 billion** of debt provide a net liquidity cushion that is easy to overlook when focusing on headline price-to-earnings alone.
